creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Best Travel Destination in Denmark 🇩🇰

Bornholm is an incredible place to go on an active holiday in Denmark 🙌

Where to stay? 🏨 
I suggest looking for accommodation in towns like Gudhjem, Allinge, Sandvig, or Svaneke. Avoid Rønne, even if it’s the largest city (it’s quite dull and quite far from the main things to do).

Things to do & must-see spots 🏞️ 
📍Hammershus 
📍Sorte Gryde (The Black Pot)
📍Opalsøen (The Opal Lake)
📍Eat at a “røgeri” (a smokehouse with local seafood)
📍Døndalen
📍Dueodde Beach
📍Svaneke Bryghus
📍Jons Kapel
📍Bisonskoven 📍Rokkestenen
📍Brændesgårdshaven (awesome if you’re travelling with children)
📍Pissebækken
📍Fru Petersen’s Café

#bornholm #denmarktravel #traveltips

transcription.text: This is the best place to visit in Denmark. Denmark is a decent little country but our nature is nothing special compared to our neighboring countries apart from this place. So about three hours by bus and boat from Copenhagen you'll find the stunning island of Bornholm. They serve up some mad fish dishes over here but you'll also find one of the best preserved medieval castles in Europe, some incredible hiking trails and a beach that Lonely Planet called one of the top 20 best beaches in Europe. I think my favorite place in Bornholm is the Opel lake where you can clip, jump, go on a massive zip line and even meet a cute little lizard if you're lucky. But there's definitely enough to see and do here for at least three nights. So if you want to experience Denmark outside of Copenhagen there's no better place to travel than Bornholm.
